- impeccable: Use when the user wants to design, redesign, shape, critique, audit, polish, clarify, distill, harden, optimize, adapt, animate, colorize, extract, or otherwise improve a frontend interface. Covers websites, landing pages, dashboards, product UI, app shells, components, forms, settings, onboarding, and empty states. Handles UX review, visual hierarchy, information architecture, cognitive load, accessibility, performance, responsive behavior, theming, anti-patterns, typography, fonts, spacing, layout, alignment, color, motion, micro-interactions, UX copy, error states, edge cases, i18n, and reusable design systems or tokens. Also use for bland designs that need to become bolder or more delightful, loud designs that should become quieter, live browser iteration on UI elements, or ambitious visual effects that should feel technically extraordinary. Not for backend-only or non-UI tasks.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

1	# hChat
2	
3	A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.
4	
5	Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [Ollama](https://ollama.com) at `localhost:11434`.
6	
7	## Features
8	
9	- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
10	- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
11	- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
12	- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
13	- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
14	- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
15	- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
16	- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
17	- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
18	- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
19	- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
20	- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
21	- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
22	- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
23	- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
24	- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
25	- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
26	- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
27	- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
28	- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
29	- Works with OpenRouter, LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible API
30	- Hover timestamps on messages
31	- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
32	- Dark/light theme toggle
33	- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
34	- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
35	- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)
36	
37	## Install
38	
39	Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).
40	
41	### macOS (Homebrew)
42	
43	```bash
44	brew tap heath0xFF/tap
45	brew install hchat
46	```
47	
48	To update to the latest release:
49	
50	```bash
51	brew update && brew upgrade hchat
52	```
53	
54	### macOS (manual)
55	
56	```bash
57	# Binary
58	tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
59	mv hchat /usr/local/bin/
60	
61	# Or use the .app bundle
62	unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
63	```
64	
65	### Debian/Ubuntu
66	
67	```bash
68	sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
69	```
70	
71	### Arch Linux
72	
73	```bash
74	# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
75	makepkg -si
76	```
77	
78	## Build from source
79	
80	Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).
81	
82	```bash
83	cargo run --release
84	```
85	
86	## Launch
87	
88	Make sure your local LLM server is running first:
89	
90	```bash
91	ollama serve
92	```
93	
94	Then launch hChat:
95	
96	```bash
97	# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
98	hchat &disown
99	
100	# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
101	open /Applications/hChat.app
102	# or
103	hchat &disown
104	```
105	
106	hChat connects to `http://localhost:11434/v1` by default. You can change the endpoint in the top bar.
107	
108	### Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs
109	
110	1. Click `+` next to the endpoint selector in the top bar
111	2. Enter the API base URL (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`) and your API key
112	3. Click Add, then select the new endpoint from the dropdown
113	4. Models auto-populate from the remote API
114	
115	Or configure it directly in `config.toml` — see [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for all options.
116	
117	## Configuration
118	
119	Two layers:
120	
121	- **Global defaults** live in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — model name, system prompt, sampling params, endpoints, fonts, theme. These seed every new conversation. Edit the file directly or use the settings panel (gear icon). See [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for a fully commented example.
122	- **Per-conversation overrides** live in the SQLite database alongside your messages. Tweaking the system prompt or temperature inside a chat only affects *that* chat — your global defaults stay untouched. Save the resulting bundle as a preset to apply elsewhere.
123	
124	Conversation data lives in `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) or `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux). Schema migrations run automatically on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data.
125	
126	All config fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.
127	
128	### Custom fonts
129	
130	Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.
131	
132	### API keys
133	
134	API keys are stored per-endpoint in `config.toml`. Endpoints that don't need authentication (like local Ollama) simply omit the `api_key` field. Keys are sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers.
135	
136	## Tools
137	
138	Tool-capable models (OpenAI gpt-4+, Claude, most modern frontier models) can call functions hChat exposes. Five defaults are seeded into `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch:
139	
140	| Tool | Safety | Description |
141	|---|---|---|
142	| `read_file` | auto | Reads a file (with optional `offset`/`limit` for slicing). Up to 100KB per call. |
143	| `list_directory` | auto | Lists entries with `d/` (directory) or `f/` (file) prefix. |
144	| `search_files` | auto | Recursive regex search; skips dotdirs and binary files. |
145	| `write_file` | confirm | Writes a file (overwrite or append). Creates parent dirs. |
146	| `run_shell` | confirm | Runs `sh -c` in the conversation's working directory. 5 minute wall-clock cap. |
147	
148	`safety = "auto"` tools execute silently; `safety = "confirm"` tools pop an approval card with the full args before running. The card has **Approve**, **Approve all in this conv** (per-conversation allowlist for that tool name), and **Reject** buttons.
149	
150	### Working directory
151	
152	Each conversation has its own `working_dir` (settings panel). All tool calls resolve relative paths against it. Defaults to your home directory; set it to a project root and the model can reason about that codebase end-to-end.
153	
154	### Defining your own tools
155	
156	Drop a `.toml` file into `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. The minimum is `name`, `description`, JSON-schema `parameters`, and a `handler`. Two handler types:
157	
158	```toml
159	# Builtin: hardcoded Rust handler. Used by the 5 defaults.
160	handler = "builtin:read_file"
161	
162	# Shell: forks an argv with {{name}} substitution from the call's arguments.
163	handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
164	safety = "confirm"  # or "auto" for read-only tools
165	```
166	
167	Restart hChat to load new tools. Edits to existing tool files take effect on next launch.
168	
169	### Iteration cap
170	
171	The model can chain tool calls — read a file, look at the imports, read those, then propose an edit. hChat caps this at **8 cycles per user turn** to prevent runaway loops. The counter resets on the next user message (or on regenerate / edit).
172	
173	## Slash commands
174	
175	Type any of these in the input box to control hChat without leaving the keyboard. Aliases in parens.
176	
177	| Command | Action |
178	|---|---|
179	| `/model <name>` (`/m`) | Switch model — argument is substring-matched against your model list |
180	| `/temp <0..2>` (`/t`) | Set sampling temperature |
181	| `/system <text>` (`/sys`) | Set the system prompt for the current conversation (empty argument clears it) |
182	| `/clear` (`/new`) | Start a new conversation |
183	| `/copy` | Copy the last assistant reply to clipboard |
184	| `/help` (`/?`, `/h`) | Show the command reference |
185	
186	Unknown commands surface as a toast rather than being sent to the model, so a typo like `/temprature 0.5` won't reach your provider.
187	
188	## Keybindings
189	
190	| Key | Action |
191	|---|---|
192	| Enter | Send message |
193	| Shift+Enter | New line |
194	| Ctrl/Cmd+N | New conversation |
195	| Ctrl/Cmd+F | Toggle find-in-conversation |
196	| Esc | Close find bar |
197	
198	Shortcuts are gated on whether a text field is currently focused — if you're typing in a settings field or message edit, Ctrl+F/Ctrl+N won't fire until you defocus.
199

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [Ollama](https://ollama.com) at `localhost:11434`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
- Works with OpenRouter, LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Hover timestamps on messages
- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

Make sure your local LLM server is running first:

```bash
ollama serve
```

Then launch hChat:

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:11434/v1` by default. You can change the endpoint in the top bar.

### Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs

1. Click `+` next to the endpoint selector in the top bar
2. Enter the API base URL (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`) and your API key
3. Click Add, then select the new endpoint from the dropdown
4. Models auto-populate from the remote API

Or configure it directly in `config.toml` — see [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for all options.

## Configuration

Two layers:

- **Global defaults** live in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — model name, system prompt, sampling params, endpoints, fonts, theme. These seed every new conversation. Edit the file directly or use the settings panel (gear icon). See [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for a fully commented example.
- **Per-conversation overrides** live in the SQLite database alongside your messages. Tweaking the system prompt or temperature inside a chat only affects *that* chat — your global defaults stay untouched. Save the resulting bundle as a preset to apply elsewhere.

Conversation data lives in `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) or `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux). Schema migrations run automatically on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data.

All config fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

### Custom fonts

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

### API keys

API keys are stored per-endpoint in `config.toml`. Endpoints that don't need authentication (like local Ollama) simply omit the `api_key` field. Keys are sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers.

## Tools

Tool-capable models (OpenAI gpt-4+, Claude, most modern frontier models) can call functions hChat exposes. Five defaults are seeded into `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch:

| Tool | Safety | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `read_file` | auto | Reads a file (with optional `offset`/`limit` for slicing). Up to 100KB per call. |
| `list_directory` | auto | Lists entries with `d/` (directory) or `f/` (file) prefix. |
| `search_files` | auto | Recursive regex search; skips dotdirs and binary files. |
| `write_file` | confirm | Writes a file (overwrite or append). Creates parent dirs. |
| `run_shell` | confirm | Runs `sh -c` in the conversation's working directory. 5 minute wall-clock cap. |

`safety = "auto"` tools execute silently; `safety = "confirm"` tools pop an approval card with the full args before running. The card has **Approve**, **Approve all in this conv** (per-conversation allowlist for that tool name), and **Reject** buttons.

### Working directory

Each conversation has its own `working_dir` (settings panel). All tool calls resolve relative paths against it. Defaults to your home directory; set it to a project root and the model can reason about that codebase end-to-end.

### Defining your own tools

Drop a `.toml` file into `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. The minimum is `name`, `description`, JSON-schema `parameters`, and a `handler`. Two handler types:

```toml
# Builtin: hardcoded Rust handler. Used by the 5 defaults.
handler = "builtin:read_file"

# Shell: forks an argv with {{name}} substitution from the call's arguments.
handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
safety = "confirm"  # or "auto" for read-only tools
```

Restart hChat to load new tools. Edits to existing tool files take effect on next launch.

### Iteration cap

The model can chain tool calls — read a file, look at the imports, read those, then propose an edit. hChat caps this at **8 cycles per user turn** to prevent runaway loops. The counter resets on the next user message (or on regenerate / edit).

## Slash commands

Type any of these in the input box to control hChat without leaving the keyboard. Aliases in parens.

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `/model <name>` (`/m`) | Switch model — argument is substring-matched against your model list |
| `/temp <0..2>` (`/t`) | Set sampling temperature |
| `/system <text>` (`/sys`) | Set the system prompt for the current conversation (empty argument clears it) |
| `/clear` (`/new`) | Start a new conversation |
| `/copy` | Copy the last assistant reply to clipboard |
| `/help` (`/?`, `/h`) | Show the command reference |

Unknown commands surface as a toast rather than being sent to the model, so a typo like `/temprature 0.5` won't reach your provider.

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl/Cmd+N | New conversation |
| Ctrl/Cmd+F | Toggle find-in-conversation |
| Esc | Close find bar |

Shortcuts are gated on whether a text field is currently focused — if you're typing in a settings field or message edit, Ctrl+F/Ctrl+N won't fire until you defocus.

content.text:
My take on host detection: skip it. The existing connection-refused errors already tell the user what's wrong, and adding `localhost:1234` → "is LM Studio running?" is noise that doesn't generalize (people run LM Studio on non-default ports, behind reverse proxies, on LAN IPs).

What would actually be useful, if you want a small LM Studio-flavored polish: when `fetch_models` returns *success but empty*, surface a hint ("no models loaded at this endpoint — load one in LM Studio first"). That's the real LM Studio gotcha — the server runs fine but `/v1/models` is empty until you click "Load" on a model. Want me to add that too, or keep this PR purely about defaults?

I'll proceed with the defaults swap first.

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [Ollama](https://ollama.com) at `localhost:11434`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
- Works with OpenRouter, LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Hover timestamps on messages
- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
```

```bash
# or
```

1. Click `+` next to the endpoint selector in the top bar
2. Enter the API base URL (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`) and your API key
3. Click Add, then select the new endpoint from the dropdown
4. Models auto-populate from the remote API

- **Per-conversation overrides** live in the SQLite database alongside your messages. Tweaking the system prompt or temperature inside a chat only affects *that* chat — your global defaults stay untouched. Save the resulting bundle as a preset to apply elsewhere.

Conversation data lives in `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) or `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux). Schema migrations run automatically on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data.

All config fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

### API keys

API keys are stored per-endpoint in `config.toml`. Endpoints that don't need authentication (like local Ollama) simply omit the `api_key` field. Keys are sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers.

Tool-capable models (OpenAI gpt-4+, Claude, most modern frontier models) can call functions hChat exposes. Five defaults are seeded into `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch:

| Tool | Safety | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `read_file` | auto | Reads a file (with optional `offset`/`limit` for slicing). Up to 100KB per call. |
| `list_directory` | auto | Lists entries with `d/` (directory) or `f/` (file) prefix. |
| `search_files` | auto | Recursive regex search; skips dotdirs and binary files. |
| `write_file` | confirm | Writes a file (overwrite or append). Creates parent dirs. |
| `run_shell` | confirm | Runs `sh -c` in the conversation's working directory. 5 minute wall-clock cap. |

`safety = "auto"` tools execute silently; `safety = "confirm"` tools pop an approval card with the full args before running. The card has **Approve**, **Approve all in this conv** (per-conversation allowlist for that tool name), and **Reject** buttons.

### Working directory

Each conversation has its own `working_dir` (settings panel). All tool calls resolve relative paths against it. Defaults to your home directory; set it to a project root and the model can reason about that codebase end-to-end.

### Defining your own tools

Drop a `.toml` file into `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. The minimum is `name`, `description`, JSON-schema `parameters`, and a `handler`. Two handler types:

```toml

handler = "builtin:read_file"

# Shell: forks an argv with {{name}} substitution from the call's arguments.
handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
safety = "confirm"  # or "auto" for read-only tools
```

Restart hChat to load new tools. Edits to existing tool files take effect on next launch.

The model can chain tool calls — read a file, look at the imports, read those, then propose an edit. hChat caps this at **8 cycles per user turn** to prevent runaway loops. The counter resets on the next user message (or on regenerate / edit).

## Slash commands

Type any of these in the input box to control hChat without leaving the keyboard. Aliases in parens.

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `/model <name>` (`/m`) | Switch model — argument is substring-matched against your model list |
| `/temp <0..2>` (`/t`) | Set sampling temperature |
| `/system <text>` (`/sys`) | Set the system prompt for the current conversation (empty argument clears it) |
| `/clear` (`/new`) | Start a new conversation |
| `/copy` | Copy the last assistant reply to clipboard |
| `/help` (`/?`, `/h`) | Show the command reference |

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl/Cmd+N | New conversation |
| Ctrl/Cmd+F | Toggle find-in-conversation |
| Esc | Close find bar |

Shortcuts are gated on whether a text field is currently focused — if you're typing in a settings field or message edit, Ctrl+F/Ctrl+N won't fire until you defocus.

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [LM Studio](https://lmstudio.ai) at `localhost:1234`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
- Works with OpenRouter, LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Hover timestamps on messages
- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [LM Studio](https://lmstudio.ai) at `localhost:1234`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
- Works with LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, vLLM, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Hover timestamps on messages
- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

Make sure your local LLM server is running first. For LM Studio: open the app, switch to the **Developer** tab, load a model, and start the local server (defaults to port 1234). For Ollama: `ollama serve`.

Then launch hChat:

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:1234/v1` (LM Studio) by default. Add Ollama, OpenRouter, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via the `+` button in the top bar.

### Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs

1. Click `+` next to the endpoint selector in the top bar
2. Enter the API base URL (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`) and your API key
3. Click Add, then select the new endpoint from the dropdown
4. Models auto-populate from the remote API

Or configure it directly in `config.toml` — see [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for all options.

## Configuration

Two layers:

- **Global defaults** live in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — model name, system prompt, sampling params, endpoints, fonts, theme. These seed every new conversation. Edit the file directly or use the settings panel (gear icon). See [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for a fully commented example.
- **Per-conversation overrides** live in the SQLite database alongside your messages. Tweaking the system prompt or temperature inside a chat only affects *that* chat — your global defaults stay untouched. Save the resulting bundle as a preset to apply elsewhere.

Conversation data lives in `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) or `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux). Schema migrations run automatically on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data.

All config fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

### Custom fonts

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

### API keys

API keys are stored per-endpoint in `config.toml`. Endpoints that don't need authentication (like local Ollama) simply omit the `api_key` field. Keys are sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers.

## Tools

Tool-capable models (OpenAI gpt-4+, Claude, most modern frontier models) can call functions hChat exposes. Five defaults are seeded into `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch:

| Tool | Safety | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `read_file` | auto | Reads a file (with optional `offset`/`limit` for slicing). Up to 100KB per call. |
| `list_directory` | auto | Lists entries with `d/` (directory) or `f/` (file) prefix. |
| `search_files` | auto | Recursive regex search; skips dotdirs and binary files. |
| `write_file` | confirm | Writes a file (overwrite or append). Creates parent dirs. |
| `run_shell` | confirm | Runs `sh -c` in the conversation's working directory. 5 minute wall-clock cap. |

`safety = "auto"` tools execute silently; `safety = "confirm"` tools pop an approval card with the full args before running. The card has **Approve**, **Approve all in this conv** (per-conversation allowlist for that tool name), and **Reject** buttons.

### Working directory

Each conversation has its own `working_dir` (settings panel). All tool calls resolve relative paths against it. Defaults to your home directory; set it to a project root and the model can reason about that codebase end-to-end.

### Defining your own tools

Drop a `.toml` file into `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. The minimum is `name`, `description`, JSON-schema `parameters`, and a `handler`. Two handler types:

```toml
# Builtin: hardcoded Rust handler. Used by the 5 defaults.
handler = "builtin:read_file"

# Shell: forks an argv with {{name}} substitution from the call's arguments.
handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
safety = "confirm"  # or "auto" for read-only tools
```

Restart hChat to load new tools. Edits to existing tool files take effect on next launch.

### Iteration cap

The model can chain tool calls — read a file, look at the imports, read those, then propose an edit. hChat caps this at **8 cycles per user turn** to prevent runaway loops. The counter resets on the next user message (or on regenerate / edit).

## Slash commands

Type any of these in the input box to control hChat without leaving the keyboard. Aliases in parens.

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `/model <name>` (`/m`) | Switch model — argument is substring-matched against your model list |
| `/temp <0..2>` (`/t`) | Set sampling temperature |
| `/system <text>` (`/sys`) | Set the system prompt for the current conversation (empty argument clears it) |
| `/clear` (`/new`) | Start a new conversation |
| `/copy` | Copy the last assistant reply to clipboard |
| `/help` (`/?`, `/h`) | Show the command reference |

Unknown commands surface as a toast rather than being sent to the model, so a typo like `/temprature 0.5` won't reach your provider.

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl/Cmd+N | New conversation |
| Ctrl/Cmd+F | Toggle find-in-conversation |
| Esc | Close find bar |

Shortcuts are gated on whether a text field is currently focused — if you're typing in a settings field or message edit, Ctrl+F/Ctrl+N won't fire until you defocus.

content.text:
All 70 tests pass.

**Summary of the empty-models hint:**

`src/api.rs:152-220` — `fetch_models` now distinguishes three outcomes:
1. **Both paths return a non-empty model list** → return it as before
2. **OpenAI `/v1/models` returned 200 with `data: []`** (LM Studio gotcha) OR **Ollama `/api/tags` returned 200 with `models: []`** (no models pulled) → return the actionable hint: *"No models available at this endpoint. For LM Studio: load a model in the Developer tab and start the server. For Ollama: run `ollama pull <model>`."*
3. **Neither responded** → pass through the raw connection/parse error as before (so genuine network issues still surface accurately)

The hint reaches the UI through the existing `StreamEvent::Error` → `app.rs:1248` toast pipeline, replacing what was previously a silent empty dropdown at `app.rs:1204`.

Full ritual: `cargo build --release` clean, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, all 70 tests pass.

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [LM Studio](https://lmstudio.ai) at `localhost:1234`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- **Native tool calling** — the model can read files, search code, run shell commands, and write back into your working directory. Five default tools ship pre-configured (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `write_file`, `run_shell`); destructive ones prompt for approval. Define your own tools as TOML files in `~/.config/hchat/tools/` (works with any tool-capable OpenAI-compatible model)
- **Branching conversations** — every regenerate or edit creates a sibling instead of overwriting; navigate alternates with `◀ N/M ▶` arrows on any branched message
- **Multimodal content** — drag-and-drop images (png/jpg/webp/gif) into the input as attachments for vision-capable models
- **Drag-and-drop text files** (rs, py, md, json, etc.) inline into the input as fenced code blocks with language inferred from the extension
- **Per-conversation settings** — each chat owns its own model, system prompt, temperature, sampling params, and endpoint, persisted to its row
- **Presets** — save the current chat's settings as a named bundle, apply to other chats or seed new ones
- **Pinned conversations** sort to the top of the sidebar
- **Drafts** — your in-progress input persists per-conversation; switch chats and come back to find it intact
- **Auto-titled chats** — after the first assistant response, hChat generates a short conversation title via a one-shot completion against your current model
- Markdown rendering in AI responses, with **per-code-block copy buttons** and language pills
- **Reasoning model support** — inline `<think>` blocks and provider `reasoning` deltas (qwen3, deepseek-r1, gpt-oss, etc.) render as collapsible sections that auto-collapse when streaming finishes
- **Slash commands** for keyboard-first control: `/model`, `/temp`, `/system`, `/clear`, `/copy`, `/help`
- **Find in conversation** (Ctrl+F) with match highlighting and scroll-to-first-match
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence (WAL + busy_timeout). Schema migrations run on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, rename, pin, and markdown export
- **Live token counter** in the input footer (tiktoken-cached) plus post-response usage and cost display
- System prompt, temperature, max tokens — plus **advanced sampling controls** (`top_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, stop sequences)
- Multiple saved API endpoints with per-endpoint API key support
- Works with LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, vLLM, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Hover timestamps on messages
- Empty-state starter prompts to kick off new chats
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`) — corrupted files are backed up rather than silently reset
- Configurable font sizes, custom fonts, and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

Make sure your local LLM server is running first. For LM Studio: open the app, switch to the **Developer** tab, load a model, and start the local server (defaults to port 1234). For Ollama: `ollama serve`.

Then launch hChat:

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:1234/v1` (LM Studio) by default. Add Ollama, OpenRouter, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via the `+` button in the top bar.

### Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs

1. Click `+` next to the endpoint selector in the top bar
2. Enter the API base URL (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`) and your API key
3. Click Add, then select the new endpoint from the dropdown
4. Models auto-populate from the remote API

Or configure it directly in `config.toml` — see [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for all options.

## Configuration

Two layers:

- **Global defaults** live in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` — model name, system prompt, sampling params, endpoints, fonts, theme. These seed every new conversation. Edit the file directly or use the settings panel (gear icon). See [example.config.toml](example.config.toml) for a fully commented example.
- **Per-conversation overrides** live in the SQLite database alongside your messages. Tweaking the system prompt or temperature inside a chat only affects *that* chat — your global defaults stay untouched. Save the resulting bundle as a preset to apply elsewhere.

Conversation data lives in `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` (macOS) or `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db` (Linux). Schema migrations run automatically on launch, so upgrading between versions doesn't require wiping your data.

All config fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

### Custom fonts

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

### API keys

API keys are stored per-endpoint in `config.toml`. Endpoints that don't need authentication (like local LM Studio or Ollama) simply omit the `api_key` field. Keys are sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers.

## Tools

Tool-capable models (OpenAI gpt-4+, Claude, most modern frontier models) can call functions hChat exposes. Five defaults are seeded into `~/.config/hchat/tools/` on first launch:

| Tool | Safety | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `read_file` | auto | Reads a file (with optional `offset`/`limit` for slicing). Up to 100KB per call. |
| `list_directory` | auto | Lists entries with `d/` (directory) or `f/` (file) prefix. |
| `search_files` | auto | Recursive regex search; skips dotdirs and binary files. |
| `write_file` | confirm | Writes a file (overwrite or append). Creates parent dirs. |
| `run_shell` | confirm | Runs `sh -c` in the conversation's working directory. 5 minute wall-clock cap. |

`safety = "auto"` tools execute silently; `safety = "confirm"` tools pop an approval card with the full args before running. The card has **Approve**, **Approve all in this conv** (per-conversation allowlist for that tool name), and **Reject** buttons.

### Working directory

Each conversation has its own `working_dir` (settings panel). All tool calls resolve relative paths against it. Defaults to your home directory; set it to a project root and the model can reason about that codebase end-to-end.

### Defining your own tools

Drop a `.toml` file into `~/.config/hchat/tools/`. The minimum is `name`, `description`, JSON-schema `parameters`, and a `handler`. Two handler types:

```toml
# Builtin: hardcoded Rust handler. Used by the 5 defaults.
handler = "builtin:read_file"

# Shell: forks an argv with {{name}} substitution from the call's arguments.
handler = { shell = ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-n", "{{count}}"] }
safety = "confirm"  # or "auto" for read-only tools
```

Restart hChat to load new tools. Edits to existing tool files take effect on next launch.

### Iteration cap

The model can chain tool calls — read a file, look at the imports, read those, then propose an edit. hChat caps this at **8 cycles per user turn** to prevent runaway loops. The counter resets on the next user message (or on regenerate / edit).

## Slash commands

Type any of these in the input box to control hChat without leaving the keyboard. Aliases in parens.

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `/model <name>` (`/m`) | Switch model — argument is substring-matched against your model list |
| `/temp <0..2>` (`/t`) | Set sampling temperature |
| `/system <text>` (`/sys`) | Set the system prompt for the current conversation (empty argument clears it) |
| `/clear` (`/new`) | Start a new conversation |
| `/copy` | Copy the last assistant reply to clipboard |
| `/help` (`/?`, `/h`) | Show the command reference |

Unknown commands surface as a toast rather than being sent to the model, so a typo like `/temprature 0.5` won't reach your provider.

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl/Cmd+N | New conversation |
| Ctrl/Cmd+F | Toggle find-in-conversation |
| Esc | Close find bar |

Shortcuts are gated on whether a text field is currently focused — if you're typing in a settings field or message edit, Ctrl+F/Ctrl+N won't fire until you defocus.

- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Prerequisites

hChat is a client — it needs an OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint to talk to. Pick whichever you prefer:

### LM Studio (default)

1. Download from [lmstudio.ai](https://lmstudio.ai).
2. Inside LM Studio, search for and download a model (e.g. `qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct`).
3. Switch to the **Developer** tab, load the model, and start the local server.
4. The server listens on `http://localhost:1234/v1` — hChat connects there out of the box.

### Ollama

1. Install from [ollama.com](https://ollama.com) (or your package manager).
2. Pull a model: `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b`.
3. Run `ollama serve` (it auto-starts on macOS).
4. In hChat, click `+` next to the endpoint selector and add `http://localhost:11434/v1` — or set it as `default_endpoint` in `config.toml`.

### Remote APIs

OpenRouter, OpenAI, vLLM, Together, or any other OpenAI-compatible host works. You'll need the base URL and an API key — see [Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs](#using-openrouter-or-other-remote-apis) below.

If hChat can reach the endpoint but reports "No models available", you started the server but haven't loaded (LM Studio) or pulled (Ollama) a model yet.

## Install

- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Prerequisites

hChat is a client — it needs an OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint to talk to. Pick whichever you prefer:

### LM Studio (default)

1. Download from [lmstudio.ai](https://lmstudio.ai).
2. Inside LM Studio, search for and download a model (e.g. `qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct`).
3. Switch to the **Developer** tab, load the model, and start the local server.
4. The server listens on `http://localhost:1234/v1` — hChat connects there out of the box.

### Ollama

1. Install from [ollama.com](https://ollama.com) (or your package manager).
2. Pull a model: `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b`.
3. Run `ollama serve` (it auto-starts on macOS).
4. In hChat, click `+` next to the endpoint selector and add `http://localhost:11434/v1` — or set it as `default_endpoint` in `config.toml`.

### Remote APIs

OpenRouter, OpenAI, vLLM, Together, or any other OpenAI-compatible host works. You'll need the base URL and an API key — see [Using OpenRouter or other remote APIs](#using-openrouter-or-other-remote-apis) below.

If hChat can reach the endpoint but reports "No models available", you started the server but haven't loaded (LM Studio) or pulled (Ollama) a model yet.

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

With your LLM server already running (see [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)):

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:1234/v1` (LM Studio) by default. Switch endpoints from the top bar dropdown, or add new ones via the `+` button.

content.text:
Audit complete — I verified each code path against a live LM Studio instance running locally.

**What works correctly:**

| Code path | Endpoint | LM Studio result |
|---|---|---|
| `fetch_models` (api.rs:152) | `GET /v1/models` | 200, response shape matches `OpenAIModelsResponse { data: [{ id }] }` cleanly |
| `stream_chat` (api.rs:231) | `POST /v1/chat/completions` (stream) | 200, SSE format is canonical OpenAI: `data: {chunk}` lines + `data: [DONE]` terminator |
| `delta.content` parsing | piecewise content fragments | matches `Delta { content: Option<String> }` |
| `complete_once` (api.rs:542, used for auto-title) | `POST /v1/chat/completions` (non-stream) | same endpoint, same shape |

The SSE parser, the byte-level UTF-8 boundary handling, the tool_call accumulator, and the `host_is` OpenRouter gating are all neutral with respect to LM Studio — they just don't fire on a localhost host.

**One real issue I found:**

LM Studio gates `usage` in streaming responses behind `stream_options: {include_usage: true}` (same as OpenAI proper). I confirmed this with two live requests — without the flag, the stream ends with `data: [DONE]` and no usage chunk; with the flag, a final chunk arrives with `{"usage": {"prompt_tokens":10,"completion_tokens":4,"total_tokens":14,...}}`.

`ChatRequest` at `src/api.rs:46-68` does **not** send `stream_options`, so the post-response **token counter and cost display will stay empty for LM Studio streaming responses**. Non-streaming paths (auto-title) are unaffected because the usage block comes back in the body by default. Other providers handle this differently (OpenRouter emits usage by default; OpenAI also requires the flag), so unconditionally adding `stream_options: {include_usage: true}` to streaming requests is the right fix and shouldn't break anyone.

**One non-issue I noticed and verified away:**

I initially worried that a user typing `http://localhost:1234` (no `/v1`) would have `fetch_models` work but `stream_chat` 404, because `stream_chat` doesn't auto-append `/v1` the way `fetch_models` does. Turns out LM Studio happily accepts `POST /chat/completions` without the prefix (returned 200 in my test), so the asymmetry doesn't bite in practice. Not worth touching.

**Verdict:** code is correct for LM Studio. The streaming-usage gap is the only real fix worth making. Want me to add `stream_options` to streaming requests?

# --- Endpoints & Connections ---

# default_endpoint: The URL of the endpoint to use by default on application launch.
# This URL must exactly match one of the URLs in your `saved_endpoints` list below.
# Default: "http://localhost:1234/v1"
default_endpoint = "http://localhost:1234/v1"

# saved_endpoints: A list of AI providers and models you want to connect to.
# Each endpoint requires a `url` and optionally an `api_key`.
# If `api_key` is omitted, no Authorization header will be sent (perfect for local instances like LM Studio).

# Local LM Studio (No API key needed — start the server from the LM Studio app)
[[saved_endpoints]]
url = "http://localhost:1234/v1"

# Example: Ollama (Usually runs locally, no API key needed)
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

# Example: OpenRouter (Requires API key from https://openrouter.ai/keys)
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
# api_key = "sk-or-v1-your-key-here"

# Example: OpenAI or other compatible APIs
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "https://api.example.com/v1"
# api_key = "your-key-here"

feat: switch default backend to LM Studio + streaming usage fix

- Default endpoint and seeded saved_endpoint swap from Ollama to LM Studio
  (http://localhost:1234/v1). Existing user configs are preserved.
- fetch_models returns an actionable hint when an endpoint is reachable but
  reports zero models (LM Studio with no loaded model, or Ollama with
  nothing pulled) instead of silently showing an empty dropdown.
- Streaming requests opt into usage chunks via stream_options.include_usage
  so the post-response token counter and cost display populate against
  LM Studio and other OpenAI-strict providers. OpenRouter already emits
  usage by default, so this is additive there.
- README adds a Prerequisites section covering LM Studio, Ollama, and
  remote APIs; example.config.toml leads with the LM Studio seed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
